questions.question: List three common challenges faced during the Requirements Elicitation process. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Stakeholder Ambiguity: Stakeholders may not know or cannot articulate what they want.
Implicit Knowledge: Stakeholders omit information they assume is obvious.
Conflicting Viewpoints: Different stakeholders have different and competing needs.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Interview and Survey techniques for requirements elicitation. Discuss the scenarios where each would be most effective. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Definition: Interviews are direct, conversational (1-1 or small group). Surveys are written questions for a large audience.
Depth vs. Breadth: Interviews provide deep, qualitative insights. Surveys provide broad, quantitative data.
Cost & Time: Interviews are time-consuming and costly. Surveys are quick and cheap to administer.
Best For Scenario: Interviews are best for detailed understanding from key experts. Surveys are best for gathering opinions from a large, dispersed group.
Limitation: Interviews risk interviewer bias. Surveys suffer from low depth and potential low response rates.
